MDPI Energies: Performance Assessment of Hybrid Photovoltaic-Algal Bioreactor Systems
ScienceDirect: Symbiotic Mechanism of Solar Panel Cooling via Microalgae Cultivation

technical_documents.description: Stacks translucent algae tubes behind photovoltaic panels, cooling the PV cells to boost efficiency while producing lipid-rich algal biomass for biodiesel.
technical_documents.problem_statement: Solar panel efficiency drops 0.4% per degree above 25°C, costing tropical installations up to 18% annual output while algae farms require separate land and infrastructure.
technical_documents.engineering_solution: Translucent acrylic bioreactor tubes filled with Chlorella vulgaris are mounted directly behind PV panels in a shared frame; the algae absorb waste heat to grow faster while cooling the panels by 12°C, creating a symbiotic 22% net efficiency gain.

step_by_step_build: Step 1: Construct a V-frame mount with solar panel on the sun-facing side and acrylic tubes behind.
Step 2: Connect tubes in a serpentine loop with silicone elbows; fill with dechlorinated water.
Step 3: Inoculate with Chlorella starter culture and install air pump diffuser at the lowest point.
Step 4: Add nutrient solution weekly; monitor pH at 7.0–7.5 using test strips.
Step 5: Harvest algae every 14 days by diverting flow through a 50-micron mesh filter; press for oil.

comments.text: Ran this for 6 months in Thailand. Panel output went up 19% and harvested 2 kg algae biomass.
